comments.author: @Kirsten-y1c
comments.text: When I lived there we were hit by a hurricane that had the entire area knee deep in water and all roads and any bridge destroyed. No electricity nor water for 2 weeks, no communication with the outside world at all. This is when I learned water is the most important thing.

comments.author: @Kirsten-y1c
comments.text:
I had the privilege of living there for a year and it still is a part of me.
I'm grateful to have met the wonderful people.
It's not altogether Mexico like the mainland; it's a place of it's own. It IS Mexico, no doubt and anyone who didrespects that is an ignorant fool. I am in awe of the people ❤

comments.author: @infiniteplanet88
comments.text: Baja California is a breathtaking 750-mile-long peninsula where the harsh desert meets the vibrant sea. It is a land of extreme contrasts, featuring everything from towering cacti and snow-capped mountains to diverse marine life like whale sharks and migrating grey whales.

comments.text: It breaks my heart when people carelessly refer to our region simply as "Baja," or the sea beside it as the "The Sea of Cortéz." It is so important to remember its real name "California" and  "Gulf of California."

comments.author: @BeyondNationsTV
comments.text: The contrast in Baja California is incredible. Endless desert landscapes flowing directly into the deep blue ocean create such a unique and powerful visual experience. The lighting, textures, and wide-open spaces make every scene feel cinematic and alive.

comments.text: i love baja. i have been there many times, but it has been over a decade or more, time goes by. my heart yearns when i see so many familiar places that i have visited.  i want to visit there to see and experience the blooming desert once more

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: These clips are really impressive—the contrast between the sea and the desert is wild. One side is dry and scorching, while the other is this vast, endless ocean, and it makes for such a unique experience. Keep these videos coming!

comments.author: @Correcaminos2025
comments.text: Baja is still beautiful and plenty wild enough, I'm writing this from my self built, off grid home here. This former small fishing town is our alternative retirement community, it's packed with active American and Canadian seniors Nov-April pursuing kite surfing and wing foiling on the Sea of Cortes. It has taken off as an international destination for water sports and free diving, attracting a seriously hip and fit crowd of all ages, it's magical, la Pura Vida! Desde Rancho Sueño Azul, BCS
